title: Make effects exactly-once end to end
description: Combine checkpointed source offsets with idempotent or transactional sinks so a recovery neither drops nor duplicates output.
body: Use when downstream effects must not double-count after a failure. Do not claim exactly-once from engine checkpointing alone — if the sink is not transactional or idempotent, recovery replays records and duplicates writes. Actions: checkpoint source offsets with operator state, use a transactional sink (two-phase commit) or an idempotent upsert keyed on an event id, and force a restart to confirm no duplicates. Validation signals include a post-recovery output count matching the input exactly and a sink that deduplicates replays. Risk boundary: at-least-once into a non-idempotent sink corrupts totals; if the sink cannot be made transactional or idempotent, escalate before promising exactly-once.
